course.title: Advanced Applied Mathematics: Computational Systems
course.description: Learners prepared for calculus-level work who want an integrated computational path through continuous, discrete, stochastic, optimization, and research-grade modeling. This original, research-backed path uses repeated retrieval, cumulative artifacts, changed-case labs, stakeholder projects, and a performance exam.
outcomes: Demonstrate single and multivariable calculus with applications through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate linear algebra, differential equations, probability, statistics, and optimization through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.
Demonstrate numerical methods, pdes, signals, operations research, and reproducible modeling through an independently verified stakeholder artifact.

competencies.statement: Formulate identifiability in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
misconceptions: identifiability can be applied from a memorized formula without checking assumptions, conditioning, or validation.
masteryEvidence: A changed authentic task independently demonstrates this outcome: Formulate identifiability in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

credential.title: Advanced Applied Mathematics: Computational Systems Certificate

modules.title: Model Formulation, Identifiability, and Validation
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving energy demand and storage. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible model rigor computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate identifiability in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive nondimensionalization in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute calibration validation in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Vectors, Geometry, and Multivariable Functions
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving navigation and spatial sensing. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible vector geometry computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate vector spaces in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive dot cross products in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute planes surfaces in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Limits, Derivatives, and Local Approximation
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving changing biological or economic rates. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible differential calculus computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate limit models in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive derivative definition in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute derivative rules in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Integration, Accumulation, and Continuous Models
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving accumulated flow and exposure. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible integral calculus computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate riemann accumulation in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive fundamental theorem in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute integration methods in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Multivariable Calculus and Fields
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving temperature and transport fields. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible multivariable calculus computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate partial derivatives in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive gradient directional in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute multiple integrals in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Linear Systems, Spaces, and Transformations
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving coupled production and measurement systems. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible linear transformations computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate linear independence in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive basis dimension in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute matrix transformations in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Eigenvalues, Decompositions, and Dimension Reduction
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving high-dimensional sensor data. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible spectral methods computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate eigen systems in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive diagonalization in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute orthogonality qr in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Ordinary Differential Equations and Initial Values
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving population and mechanical dynamics. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible differential equations computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate first order odes in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive second order odes in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute systems odes in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Nonlinear Dynamics, Stability, and Control
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving controlled nonlinear systems. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible dynamical systems computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate phase portraits in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive equilibria stability in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute linearization systems in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Probability Models and Stochastic Processes
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving reliability and arrival processes. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible stochastic models computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate continuous random variables in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive joint conditional in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute limit theorems in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Statistical Inference, Bayesian Updating, and Prediction
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving evidence-based prediction. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible advanced statistics computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate likelihood estimation in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive interval test theory in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute bayesian models in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Numerical Error, Roots, Interpolation, and Quadrature
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving precision-sensitive scientific computation. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible numerical foundations computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate conditioning stability in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive root finding in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute interpolation splines in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Numerical Linear Algebra and Optimization
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving large constrained computational models. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible numerical linear optimization computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate direct iterative solvers in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive matrix conditioning in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute gradient methods in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Partial Differential Equations and Continuum Models
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving diffusion, waves, and transport. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible partial differential equations computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate pde classification in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive boundary initial conditions in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute separation variables in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Fourier Analysis, Signals, and Time Series
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving signals and temporal demand. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible signals fourier computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate fourier series in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive fourier transform in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute sampling aliasing in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Networks, Queues, Simulation, and Operations Research
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving routing, queues, and allocation. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible operations research computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate network flow in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive integer programming in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute queueing models in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.

modules.title: Advanced Modeling Capstone and Reproducible Research
modules.description: Build, test, and communicate a model involving an independently reviewed applied research question. Learners cumulatively produce a reproducible capstone reproducibility computational study through theory, guided work, debugging, transfer, retrieval, and assessment.
objectives: Formulate research question in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Derive computational experiment in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
Compute verification validation in a dimensioned applied model and defend the method's assumptions.
